menu.name: Plato Paceño
menu.description: A traditional dish from La Paz featuring corn on the cob, lima beans, fried cheese, and potatoes, served with beef or roasted meat.

menu.name: Sajta
menu.description: Chicken in a spicy yellow chili sauce, served with dehydrated potatoes (tunta) coated in egg and a fresh onion and tomato salad.

menu.name: Salteña
menu.description: A juicy Bolivian pastry filled with beef, potatoes, and spices, baked to golden perfection.

menu.name: kawi soup
menu.description: A delicious suop from the west of Bolivia

menu.name: Mocochinchi Drink
menu.description: A refreshing traditional beverage made from dehydrated peaches infused with cinnamon and cloves.

menu.name: Pique Macho
menu.description: A massive plate of bite-sized beef, sausages, french fries, onions, peppers, and boiled eggs topped with spicy sauce.

menu.name: Silpancho
menu.description: A thin, breaded beef schnitzel served over a bed of rice and potatoes, topped with a fried egg and beet salad.

menu.name: Chicharron
menu.description: a piece of pork 'adobado' in an special salsa

menu.name: Chancho
menu.description: a pork prepared in a cross for more thank 1 hour

menu.name: Keperi
menu.description: Made with a beef cut from the lower part of the abdomen, between the shoulder blade and the rib.

menu.name: Fricase
menu.description: Traditional 'ahogado' that consists of meat (usually chicken or pork) cut into pieces and slow-cooked.

menu.name: majadito
menu.description: A traditional food from Santa Cruz

menu.name: soup mani
menu.description: It is one of the most delicious and traditional dishes of Bolivia. It is based on peanuts, rice, peas, and potatoes. It is usually served with pieces of meat or chicken.

menu.name: Mondongo
menu.description: From Sucre, Chuquisaca, it consists of a stew made with pork meat and skin

menu.name: Picana
menu.description: A traditional food where is common make it on Christmas
